meta.one_liner: Self-hosted FastAPI + React app that splits a video into lossless fMP4 chunks, fans them out to a small replica set of free file hosts, streams them back through a range-aware proxy into an MSE player, and keeps the copies alive via a standalone expiry refresher.
meta.state: post-optimization: replica-set + concurrent uploads, progressive playback, FileDitch host integrated (free/no-auth/permanent, priority #1), Streamtape ON, the expiry refresher (standalone CLI + optional in-app schedule), and a Cloudflare Tunnel that auto-exposes the app over public HTTPS at startup.
meta.how_to_use: Load this file INSTEAD of re-reading source when you need to know what a module does, what it depends on, the data model, the API surface, or where a behavior lives. Only open the actual source file when you need exact code to edit it. After editing code, update the matching node/edge/issue here in the same change (see maintenance_protocol).
meta.maintenance_protocol: 1) Add/modify a file -> add/update its entry in `modules` (path, role, exports, imports_internal, touches_services, notes). 2) Change an import graph edge -> update `edges`. 3) Change a Pydantic/TS model -> update `data_models`. 4) Add/change a route -> update `endpoints`. 5) Change the upload/playback/resume/refresh behavior -> update `flows`. 6) Fix or introduce a perf/correctness gap -> update `known_issues` (set "status":"resolved" when fixed). 7) Bump meta.generated and, for structural changes, meta.schema_version. Keep entries terse: one line of behavior per symbol.

runtime.run_api: uvicorn backend.app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload  (run from repo root, .venv active)
runtime.run_refresher: python -m backend.app.refresher --once   |   python -m backend.app.refresher --interval 21600   (standalone process; NOT started by the API)
runtime.cloudflare_tunnel: auto-starts from the lifespan when cloudflare_tunnel_enabled (default True) AND the cloudflared binary is installed; public URL logged at startup and served by GET /api/tunnel. Quick tunnel by default (ephemeral *.trycloudflare.com); named tunnel if CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TOKEN set. Verified live 2026-06-14.
runtime.startup_lifespan: init_db() -> _check_deps(ffmpeg/ffprobe) -> resume_pending_jobs() OR clear_pending_jobs() (if CLEAR_PENDING_JOBS=1 or --clear-pending)
runtime.frontend_build: cd frontend && npm install && npm run build  -> emits frontend/dist (served at /static, /assets; dashboard.html at /dashboard)

key_urls./embed/{video_id}: standalone player page (server-rendered HTML shell that loads the Vite player bundle)
key_urls./api/stream/{video_id}/chunk/{i}: range-proxied chunk bytes (player now requests progressive sub-ranges)

tables.videos: video_id PK, name, description, duration_sec, width, height, fps, codec, upload_date, manifest_path. Search/index mirror of the manifest.
tables.stars: star_id PK, name, image_url, bio, created_at.
tables.jobs: job_id PK, status, video_id, message, total_chunks, uploaded_chunks, error, bytes_per_sec, eta_sec, created_at, updated_at. Durable upload-job state.
tables.job_checkpoints: (job_id, phase) PK, data(JSON). Resumable per-phase upload state: phases 'meta','video_info','chunks_created','chunk_{N}','streamtape_parts'. ChunkLocation is serialized with model_dump(mode='json') so datetimes round-trip.
tables.videos_fts: FTS5 mirror of videos(name,description) kept in sync by triggers videos_ai/ad/au; backs ?search=.

manifests.atomic_write: storage/manifest_store.save writes a .tmp then os.replace; the proxy and refresher never read a half-written file.
manifests.model: Manifest (backend/app/models/__init__.py). SQLite `videos` is a derived index of this.

tmp.lifecycle: upload temp kept across restarts so interrupted jobs resume; deleted on successful completion. Refresher temp deleted at end of each video pass.

config_keys.purpose: target fMP4 chunk size; chunker snaps to nearest keyframe boundary; smaller = faster start/seeks
how many host copies to keep per chunk (durability vs upload speed); upload job uploads to this many, refresher maintains it
how many chunks upload concurrently (pipelining across chunks)
max size of each browser→backend chunked-upload part; kept < Cloudflare's ~100MB body cap so uploads work through the tunnel
also split+upload the whole video to Streamtape (enables player ST mode); adds upload time
include the free/no-auth/permanent FileDitch host in the upload pool
API server also runs the refresher on a schedule in-process; refresh_initial_delay_sec (120) before the first pass
start a cloudflared tunnel from the lifespan to expose the app over public HTTPS (needs the cloudflared binary). ⚠ app has no auth
local address cloudflared forwards to (match uvicorn host:port)
if set, run a stable named tunnel instead of an ephemeral quick tunnel
loop interval for the refresher daemon
re-upload a copy this many days before estimated expiry (currently expiry is mostly handled by the keep-alive verify pass)
order hosts are tried for streaming failover and the order upload targets/replica fills are chosen
deprioritize pixeldrain (free tier rate-limits range requests)
max bytes per Streamtape full-video part
auth for each external host; missing creds -> adapter excluded from pool
discard interrupted jobs on startup instead of resuming

external_services.name: fileditch
external_services.range_support: yes (206) on a signed CDN link scraped from the viewer page; a browser User-Agent is REQUIRED (the download host 502s otherwise)
external_services.expiry_policy: PERMANENT — new.fileditch.com keeps files indefinitely; 100GB max
external_services.notes: GOTCHA: upload returns an HTML VIEWER url (fileditchfiles.me/<path>), not the raw file. download_range scrapes that page for the time-limited direct link on a separate CDN host (?md5=&expires=) and caches it. Validated live 2026-06-14.

external_services.name: gofile
external_services.range_support: limited (free tier; direct-link created per file)
external_services.expiry_policy: free files reaped after inactivity (~10 days; policy in refresher/policy.py)

external_services.name: filester
external_services.range_support: yes via short-lived CDN token (30-min token cached)
external_services.expiry_policy: AUTO-DELETES after 45 days idle; the refresher verify pass keeps it alive

external_services.name: pixeldrain
external_services.range_support: yes but free tier rate-limits; sock_read=30 timeout for fast failover
external_services.expiry_policy: free files expire after inactivity (~60d est. in policy.py)

external_services.name: streamtape
external_services.notes: served by streaming/streamtape_proxy.py; only used when streamtape_enabled

Manifest.fields: video_id, name, description, duration_sec, resolution:Resolution?, upload_date, star_ids[], thumbnail:Thumbnail, chunks:[Chunk], streamtape_parts:[StreamtapePart]
Manifest.note: persisted as JSON; source of truth

Chunk.fields: index, start_sec, end_sec, byte_size, filename, locations:[ChunkLocation]

ChunkLocation.fields: host, file_id, url, status:ok|failed|pending, uploaded_at?, last_verified_at?, expires_at?
ChunkLocation.note: expiry fields added for the refresher; Optional so pre-feature manifests still load. uploaded_at set by upload job; last_verified_at/expires_at maintained by refresher.

Resolution.fields: width, height, fps, codec, mse_codec, bitrate_kbps?

StreamtapePart.fields: index, file_id, start_sec, end_sec, byte_size, filename

JobProgress.fields: job_id, status:JobStatus, video_id?, message, total_chunks, uploaded_chunks, error?, bytes_per_sec?, eta_sec?, created_at, updated_at

StreamManifest.fields: video_id, name, description, duration_sec, resolution?, mse_codec, thumbnail, star_ids[], chunks:[StreamChunk], streamtape_parts[]

StreamChunk.fields: index, start_sec, end_sec, byte_size, hosts:[str] (ok hosts in priority order)

modules.role: FastAPI app factory, lifespan (init_db, dep check, resume/clear jobs, optional in-app refresher loop, Cloudflare tunnel start/stop), CORS, router mounts, static dist mount, /dashboard, /api/health, /api/tunnel

Cloudflare Tunnel integration. start_tunnel() spawns cloudflared from the lifespan (quick tunnel by default; named tunnel if CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TOKEN set) and scrapes the public *.trycloudflare.com URL from its output; stop_tunnel() terminates it on shutdown; get_tunnel_status() backs GET /api/tunnel. Degrades gracefully (warns) if the cloudflared binary is missing.

pydantic-settings Settings from .env; creates data dirs. Notable: replica_count(2), upload_concurrency(3), streamtape_enabled(True), fileditch_enabled(True), refresh_in_app(True)+refresh_initial_delay_sec, refresh_interval_sec, chunk_size_bytes default 6MB; stream_host_priority leads with fileditch
all Pydantic schemas (see data_models); ChunkLocation now carries expiry fields
SQLite connection factory + schema DDL + idempotent migrations
per-job checkpoint CRUD (resumable uploads)
in-process async job registry + progress persistence; cancellation via asyncio.Event

END-TO-END upload orchestration with per-phase checkpoints. Uploads each chunk to a REPLICA SET (settings.replica_count hosts, priority order, fill-on-failure) and runs chunks CONCURRENTLY (asyncio.Semaphore=upload_concurrency). Streamtape parts are OPT-IN (streamtape_enabled).
notes: throughput/ETA computed from aggregate wall-clock bytes/sec across concurrent chunks

modules.role: startup resume/clear of interrupted jobs
re-export facade
per-host idle-deletion windows (HOST_IDLE_DAYS) + estimate_expiry(host,last_activity)

refresh engine. Per chunk: VERIFY (1-byte fetch from every location = detect dead + keep-alive, updates last_verified_at/expires_at/status) then REPAIR (if healthy copies < replica_count: re-download from a healthy copy and re-upload to other hosts, rewriting ChunkLocation in place). Saves manifest atomically + reindexes.
package facade
CLI: --once | --interval | --video | --replica | --dry-run | --quiet -> run_once/run_forever
ffprobe metadata + keyframe index
lossless fMP4 chunker (-c copy)
lossless regular-MP4 splitter for Streamtape parts
detect nvenc/vaapi/cpu (informational; not on hot path)
FileHostAdapter ABC: upload / download_range / healthy / chunk_upload
instantiate adapters (FileDitch first); upload pool (chunk_upload=True, priority order) + per-chunk stream adapters + get(name)

FileDitch adapter — free, no-auth, PERMANENT. upload() returns the HTML viewer url; download_range() scrapes the viewer page (browser UA) for the signed time-limited CDN link, caches it (_link_cache keyed by viewer url, until `expires`), and Range-GETs it (re-resolves once on non-2xx). Gated by fileditch_enabled.
Gofile adapter
Filester adapter (CDN token cache)
Cyberfile adapter (token auth)
Pixeldrain adapter (Basic auth; fast failover timeout)
Streamtape adapter (whole-video parts; dlticket flow; url cache)
Buzzheavier adapter (PUT upload)
notes: ISSUE-9 (open): not wired into any upload path

modules.role: stub adapter
ImageHostAdapter ABC
Google Drive thumbnail upload (sync client in executor)
jpg.su thumbnail stub
read/write/delete per-video JSON manifests (source of truth); save() is ATOMIC (temp + os.replace)
sync a Manifest into SQLite videos + video_stars
re-export facade
single-shot POST /api/uploads + chunked upload (init/part/thumbnail/complete; _assemble_parts concatenates parts into source<suffix> in a background job), GET list, GET progress, DELETE cancel
GET /api/videos (fts/star/paged), GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/videos/{id}
stars CRUD
GET /embed/{id} -> HTML shell that injects __VM_CONFIG__ and loads the player bundle
MSE streaming: GET /manifest (StreamManifest) + GET /chunk/{i} (range proxy with multi-host failover incl. short-read detection)
GET /stream/st/{id}: maps virtual byte offset across Streamtape parts and range-proxies one part per response
resolve(timestamp,chunks)->ResolvedPosition
notes: ISSUE-10 (open): not imported server-side; player uses seek.ts

modules.role: player API types + fetchManifest + chunkUrl + streamtapeProxyUrl
resolve(timestamp,chunks)->ResolvedPosition (mirror of backend resolver)

MSE engine: sequential worker streams each chunk via fetch+ReadableStream reader and appends slices PROGRESSIVELY (timestampOffset set only on first slice of a chunk); per-chunk in-memory cache (full buffer assembled from slices); seek cancels+restarts; PREFETCH_AHEAD=3
notes: ISSUE-4 resolved: progressive append means playback starts before the whole chunk arrives
ISSUE-5 mitigated: still one worker, but progressive append + smaller chunks remove the worst stalls

ISSUE-12 resolved: onSeeking calls sb.abort() to reset the segment parser (a seek interrupting a progressive append left it mid-segment, making the next timestampOffset set throw); drainQueue retries timestampOffset after abort() as a safety net

modules.role: player UI; loads manifest; MSE vs Streamtape toggle (ST button when streamtape_parts present); keyboard shortcuts; controls
scrubber with buffered-range painting
quality badge component
notes: not imported by VideoPlayer -> possibly legacy

modules.role: spinner component
notes: not imported by VideoPlayer -> possibly legacy

modules.role: player entry (index.html)
embed bundle entry (vite.embed.config.ts)
dashboard API client incl. chunked upload (initUpload/uploadPart/uploadThumbnail/completeUpload)
dashboard shell
drag-drop upload form; slices the file into part_size blobs and uploads via init→part×N→(thumbnail)→complete (chunked, tunnel-safe), then bootstraps the JobCard
live job progress card (polls getJob; cancel)
video library grid (open player, delete)
dashboard entry (dashboard.html)

endpoints.purpose: single-shot multipart ingest (CLI/localhost); save to tmp, enqueue job; returns {job_id, video_id}
begin a chunked upload; returns {upload_id, part_size}
stream one raw file part to disk (each < Cloudflare cap)
upload the thumbnail for a chunked session
validate parts, then reassemble + enqueue job in the background (returns {job_id} immediately)
range-proxied chunk bytes with host failover

upload: routers/uploads.start_upload: stream video+thumb to data/tmp/{video_id}/, build VideoMeta, enqueue(_job_coro), persist video_id

UPLOAD: _upload_one per chunk under asyncio.Semaphore(upload_concurrency); _upload_chunk uploads to settings.replica_count hosts (priority order leads with fileditch, fill on failure); checkpoint 'chunk_{i}' = [ChunkLocation]; aggregate bytes/sec + ETA
STREAMTAPE (streamtape_enabled, default True): split_video into <=8GB parts -> upload sequentially -> [StreamtapePart]; checkpoint 'streamtape_parts'
FINALIZE: build Manifest -> storage.save (atomic JSON) + storage.upsert (SQLite) -> done -> clear_checkpoints + _cleanup_temp
chunked_upload: Used by the dashboard (works through the Cloudflare tunnel — each request stays < ~100MB).
Browser slices the File into part_size blobs; PUT /api/uploads/{id}/part/{i} streams each to parts/part_{i:06d} (one request per part)

POST /api/uploads/{id}/complete {total_parts}: validates contiguous parts, then enqueues a job that (background) _assemble_parts concatenates parts -> source<suffix>, removes parts, and runs the normal upload pipeline. Returns {job_id} immediately so the tunnel response timeout isn't hit.
playback_mse: GET /embed/{id} -> HTML shell loads player bundle
VideoPlayer.fetchManifest -> GET /manifest (ok hosts per chunk in priority order)
useChunkStream: open MediaSource, addSourceBuffer(mse_codec), start sequential worker at chunk 0

worker: GET /chunk/{i}, read response body via ReadableStream; push each slice to append queue (timestampOffset set on first slice only); proxy.stream_chunk serves bytes from first ok host with failover
playback begins after the first slice (not the whole chunk); full chunk assembled into the cache for re-seeks
seek: seek.resolve target chunk; cancel worker (gen + AbortController); flush SourceBuffer if not buffered; restart worker at target
playback_streamtape: VideoPlayer ST toggle sets <video src>=/stream/st/{id}; browser Range -> streamtape_proxy maps virtual offset to (part, local offset) -> StreamtapeAdapter ticket+CDN range -> one part per response
resume: lifespan -> resume_pending_jobs: select non-terminal jobs; require 'meta' + tmp source; rebuild JobProgress+VideoMeta; enqueue; _run skips checkpointed phases
expiry_refresh: Standalone: python -m backend.app.refresher (--once or loop). ALSO runs in-process from the API lifespan when refresh_in_app=True (main._run_refresher_loop waits refresh_initial_delay_sec then run_forever).
VERIFY each ChunkLocation: _verify_location fetches 1 byte (detects dead links AND resets idle-deletion timers = keep-alive); set status, last_verified_at, expires_at(=estimate_expiry from policy.py)

REPAIR: if healthy copies < replica_count -> _download_chunk from a healthy copy to temp, _upload_copy to other hosts (priority order, not already used), set chunk.locations = healthy + new copies (drops dead ones, rewrites file_id/url)
save manifest atomically + upsert SQLite; the streaming proxy immediately serves the refreshed URLs

known_issues.resolution: upload_job._upload_chunk now uploads to settings.replica_count hosts (default 2) instead of all; fills further hosts only on failure
Streamtape is now a toggle (streamtape_enabled). User enabled it (default True) for the player ST mode, accepting the extra upload time; set STREAMTAPE_ENABLED=0 to skip
useChunkStream streams the chunk response via ReadableStream and appends slices progressively; playback starts before the whole chunk arrives. chunk_size_bytes default also lowered to 6MB

known_issues.note: still a single sequential worker; progressive append + smaller chunks remove the worst stalls. Could add parallel range fetches later if needed

known_issues.symptom: repeat plays/seeks re-hit slow rate-limited hosts
known_issues.note: user opted not to add a proxy cache; the per-chunk in-memory cache covers within-session re-seeks
known_issues.fix_idea: optional on-disk LRU chunk cache in streaming/proxy.py

known_issues.resolution: chunk_size_bytes default set to 6MB and documented; .env still overrides (currently ~8MB)

backend/app/refresher: verify pass keeps idle-expiry hosts alive and detects dead links; repair pass re-downloads from a healthy copy and re-uploads to maintain replica_count, rewriting ChunkLocation file_id/url. ChunkLocation gained uploaded_at/last_verified_at/expires_at

known_issues.symptom: Buzzheavier configured but never uploaded to
known_issues.fix_idea: wire into replica fill path or remove
probe host health once per job and skip dead hosts

known_issues.symptom: after seeking to a new timestamp the target chunk downloads but never plays (initial playback fine)
known_issues.root_cause: the progressive-append change (ISSUE-4) could be interrupted mid-chunk by a seek, leaving the SourceBuffer parser mid-media-segment; the next sb.timestampOffset assignment then threw InvalidStateError, so appendBuffer was skipped and the chunk's data (incl. its init segment) was dropped
known_issues.resolution: useChunkStream.onSeeking calls sb.abort() to reset the parser before flush/re-append; drainQueue retries timestampOffset after abort() as a safety net

glossary.fMP4: fragmented MP4 (frag_keyframe+empty_moov+faststart+default_base_moof) so each chunk is independently appendable to an MSE SourceBuffer
glossary.MSE: Media Source Extensions; JS feeds media bytes to <video> via SourceBuffer
glossary.chunk: a lossless fMP4 slice (~chunk_size_bytes) uploaded to chunk hosts and streamed by /chunk/{i}
glossary.part: a lossless regular MP4 slice (<=8GB) uploaded only to Streamtape (opt-in) and stitched by the ST proxy
glossary.location: one (host, file_id, url, status, expiry fields) record for where a chunk's bytes live; a chunk has `replica_count` of them
glossary.replica_count: how many host copies of each chunk to keep; set on upload and maintained by the refresher
glossary.manifest: data/manifests/{id}.json; the source of truth; SQLite videos is a derived index
glossary.refresher: standalone process (python -m backend.app.refresher) that verifies/keeps-alive/repairs chunk copies
